problem: Focused audio cue sweeps can find local candidates that look promising in isolation, then fail once placed in the full Aurora theme and event-gap scoring context.
strategy: Replace one cue in the latest full-theme metrics with a representative candidate capture, run the normal event-gap scorer against that synthetic theme, and block runtime promotion unless the cue and aggregate guardrails improve.
successMeasure: A candidate may be promoted only when focused keeper gates, full-theme cue gap, worst-segment risk, theme average risk, and semantic score all hold or improve.

candidate.keeperRead: masking separation 2.85 < 4; risk stability sd 1.108 > 0.35; centroid stability sd 277.1 Hz > 120 Hz; zero-crossing stability sd 693.3/s > 420/s; band-shape stability sd 0.1403 > 0.045; RMS stability sd 0.042 > 0.035

blockers: No Formation Pulse candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

currentCueRow.label: Formation Pulse
currentCueRow.focus: Compare ongoing formation cadence and whether gameplay feels like it has enough marching pressure.
currentCueRow.worstSegmentInterpretation: onset timbre is the leading gap: band shape/rolloff differ from the reference segment.
currentCueRow.referenceSegmentationResolution: auto energy segmentation with role-by-role comparison
currentCueRow.playerMeaning: Compare ongoing formation cadence and whether gameplay feels like it has enough marching pressure.
currentCueRow.mappingStatus: Strongest current reference for the board cadence bed.
currentCueRow.semanticRecommendation: Keep semantic mapping as a guardrail while acoustic/timing gaps are tuned.
currentCueRow.recommendation: Tune the onset segment first: onset timbre is the leading gap: band shape/rolloff differ from the reference segment.

nextStep: Do not promote stagePulse; use this precheck evidence to pivot toward a stronger candidate family or the current highest-risk cue (stagePulse).
